items.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
items.summary: In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

items.title: The Google Pixel 10 Is $150 Off
items.summary: Amazon marks Google's flagship down to just $650 for 128 GB or $750 for 256 GB.

items.title: Tech Companies Are Trying to Neuter Colorado’s Landmark Right-to-Repair Law
items.summary: A bill in Colorado is a glimpse into the future of how corporations are working to limit the freedom people have to make their own fixes and upgrades.

items.title: Amazon hits sellers with ‘fuel surcharge’ as Iran war roils global energy markets
items.summary: The e-commerce giant called the surcharge "temporary" but couldn't give a date for when the policy would be retired.

items.title: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers says its customer support system was hacked
items.summary: The U.S. telehealth giant says hackers stole customer support ticket data over the course of several days in February.

items.title: Artemis II is NASA’s last moon mission without Silicon Valley
items.summary: Next time around, the pressure will be on SpaceX and Blue Origin.

items.title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
items.summary: Bondi's time as America's top law enforcement officer was overshadowed by the justice department's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein investigation.

items.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
items.summary: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

items.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
items.summary: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

items.title: Blue Owl caps private credit funds redemptions at 5% after steep request levels
items.summary: Blue Owl attributed the higher-than-usual requests to "heightened market concerns around AI-related disruption to software companies."

items.title: Visa launches new AI tools to manage the charge dispute process
items.summary: Visa's new tools are part of a larger push by major banks and financial institutions to incorporate AI into their businesses.

items.title: Warren Buffett says Iran bomb would make nuclear disaster harder to avoid
items.summary: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman said the growing number of nuclear-armed states has fundamentally altered the global risk landscape.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Hegseth lifts ban on service members carrying personal firearms on base
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Thursday that he was lifting a ban on service members’ ability to carry personal firearms on U.S. military bases. “Our warfighters defend the rights to carry. They should be able to carry themselves,” Hegseth said in a video posted on social platform X. Hegseth said his memo directs installation commanders…

items.title: ‘Bob’s Burgers’ actor rescued from fiery crash by New Hampshire governor’s security detail
items.summary: A voice actor on the popular animation series “Bob’s Burgers” was seriously injured after crashing his vehicle into a toll plaza in New Hampshire on Tuesday, according to New Hampshire State Police. Eurgene Mirman, 51, was traveling northbound on the Everett Turnpike shortly before noon on March 31 when he struck the Bedford Toll Plaza.…

items.title: 5 notable moments from Pam Bondi’s tenure as attorney general
items.summary: The Pam Bondi era at the Department of Justice (DOJ) is coming to an end, less than 14 months after it began.  President Trump announced Bondi’s departure from the DOJ on Thursday, after she spent her tenure frequently defending the president and overseeing the rollout of files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. “Pam…

items.title: Ancient bone dice reveal 12,000-year history of gambling in America
items.summary: More than 12,000 years ago, Native American hunter-gatherers were already making and using dice—thousands of years before similar tools appeared elsewhere. These bone “binary lots” acted like primitive coins, producing random outcomes for games of chance. A new study shows these weren’t accidental objects but carefully designed tools used across many regions and cultures.

items.title: NASA launches Artemis II for first crewed Moon flyby in 50 years
items.summary: A new era of lunar exploration has begun as NASA launches four astronauts on Artemis II—the first crewed mission to fly around the Moon in over 50 years. Riding aboard the powerful SLS rocket, the Orion spacecraft is now on a 10-day journey that will test critical systems, push human spaceflight farther than it’s gone in decades, and set the stage for future Moon landings and eventual missions to Mars.

items.title: Breakthrough could protect the vision cells that let you see faces and colors
items.summary: Scientists have taken a major step toward protecting the very cells that make sharp, colorful vision possible. By testing more than 2,700 compounds in thousands of lab-grown human retinal models, researchers uncovered several molecules that can shield cone photoreceptors—the cells responsible for reading, recognizing faces, and seeing color—from degeneration. They also identified a key protective mechanism involving casein kinase 1, offering a promising new target for treatment.

items.title: Fossils reveal many complex animals existed before the Cambrian explosion
items.summary: Hundreds of Chinese fossils from the dawn of animal evolution may change how scientists think of this critical period of prehistory.

items.title: To climb trees, cicadas look to the shadows
items.summary: Tree-climbing cicadas find their perches by looking for patches of darkness, a strategy known as skototaxis.

items.title: The oldest known dice date back about 12,000 years in North America
items.summary: A study of ancient artifacts suggests Native American dice games began thousands of years earlier than previously documented.
